problem: AI application development is fragmented and brittle. Teams stitch together LLM orchestration, retrieval, evaluation, and observability across ad-hoc scripts and incompatible tools. This slows experimentation, causes regression risks when models/data change, and makes it hard to ship reliable AI features into production.
solution: FoundryFlow is an AI-native developer platform that unifies orchestration, evaluation, and observability for LLM applications. Developers define workflows as a type-safe graph DSL with versioned datasets, policy guardrails, and first-class evaluation. The runtime compiles graphs to fast, reproducible microservices with built-in experiment tracking and rollback.
technology_advantage: Graph-based runtime written in Rust for low-latency, deterministic execution
Type-safe workflow DSL with Python SDK and LangChain-compatible adapters
Built-in evaluation harness (offline/online) with dataset versioning and metric packs (latency, toxicity, factuality)
Production observability: traces, prompts, and features captured for replay and root-cause analysis
Reproducible environments and policy guardrails for safe deployment across staging/production

market_opportunity.beachhead: Product and platform teams building LLM-powered features in SaaS and enterprise applications
market_opportunity.tam_estimate: Approximately $10B by 2028 for AI application tooling and orchestration
market_opportunity.sam_estimate: $1.5B near-term for orchestration, eval, and observability platforms
market_opportunity.notes: Bottom-up adoption via open-source and community; land in teams building retrieval-augmented generation, agents, and evaluation pipelines, expand to platform standard.

founders: CEO: ex-Stripe infrastructure lead (7 years), built internal workflow orchestration platform
CTO: ex-OpenAI evaluation engineer, led eval tooling and dataset pipelines
CPO: ex-HashiCorp PM, launched developer platforms and open-source programs
team.previous_outcomes: Prior devtools startup by CTO acquired in 2021

technology_stack: Adapters for LangChain, LlamaIndex, and OpenAI/Azure/HuggingFace endpoints
